title: Teeth are the only part of your body that can't heal themselves.
short: Every other tissue in your body — skin, bone, muscle — can repair itself. Teeth are the one exception. Once damaged, they stay damaged forever.
twist: The hardest thing in your body — and the most fragile.
more: Teeth are made of enamel — the hardest substance in the human body — but unlike bone, enamel contains no living cells. That means once it's cracked, chipped or worn down, your body has absolutely no way to fix it. Every dentist visit exists because of this one biological flaw. Evolution gave us the hardest material possible, but forgot to make it renewable.

title: We have better maps of Mars than of Earth's ocean floor.
short: Over 80% of Earth's oceans remain unmapped and unexplored. We know more about the surface of Mars than what lies beneath our own seas.
twist: We know more about Mars than our own backyard.
more: NASA has mapped Mars at a resolution of about 20 meters per pixel. Our ocean floor? Most of it is mapped at 5 kilometers per pixel — meaning a feature the size of a mountain could be completely invisible. The deep ocean is so hard to explore that we've sent more people to the Moon than to the deepest point of the Mariana Trench. And the Trench is only 11 km away — not 225 million km like Mars.

title: A bolt of lightning is five times hotter than the surface of the Sun.
short: The Sun's surface burns at around 5,500°C. A single lightning bolt reaches 30,000°C — over five times hotter — in a fraction of a second.
twist: Thunder is just lightning being dramatic about how hot it is.
more: The insane heat of a lightning bolt lasts only about 30 microseconds — but in that time it superheats the surrounding air so fast that the air expands explosively. That explosion is thunder. The light travels to you instantly; the sound takes about 3 seconds per kilometer. Lightning also strikes Earth about 100 times every single second — meaning by the time you finish reading this sentence, over 300 bolts have hit the ground somewhere.

title: Elephants are the only animals that can't jump.
short: Every mammal on Earth can jump — except the elephant. Not because they're too heavy, but because of how their legs and skeleton are built.
twist: Built like a tank. Runs like someone's dad.
more: Elephants' leg bones are positioned almost vertically — like pillars — which makes them incredibly strong for supporting their 6-tonne body weight, but useless for the explosive push needed to jump. They also have very little ankle flexibility. The interesting side effect? Elephants run differently from every other animal. Instead of a full gallop where all four feet leave the ground, they always keep at least one foot on the floor — meaning they technically fast-walk rather than run.

title: Your brain is more active when you're asleep than when you're watching TV.
short: Passive TV watching requires almost no cognitive effort. During deep sleep, your brain is furiously processing memories, clearing toxins and repairing itself.
twist: Your brain does more while you sleep than while you scroll.
more: While you sleep, your brain cycles through several stages — including REM sleep where it replays the day's events and consolidates memories. It also activates the glymphatic system, which flushes out toxic waste products that build up during the day, including proteins linked to Alzheimer's disease. Watching TV, by contrast, puts your brain into a low-frequency passive state. In other words: sleep is a workout. TV is a nap for your brain — but not the useful kind.
